nodes.label: Mutating tool approval (one command at a time)
nodes.rationale: Host asks approval for mutating tools one command at a time (accept/reject/always) so free-text chat is not pure autocomplete and user controls side effects.
nodes.norm_label: mutating tool approval (one command at a time)

nodes.label: Launch-cwd vs install-tree isolation
nodes.rationale: Keys stay only in MultiAgent .env; System A Git and chat file tools target launch cwd, not install tree; consumer projects need no MultiAgent files.

nodes.label: Vibe Coding safeguards
nodes.rationale: Path traversal blocked; all-or-nothing atomic writes; dirty-tree WIP stash with restore after commit/rollback; merge into existing files when possible to preserve helpers.

nodes.label: Deep Research safeguards
nodes.rationale: Query size limits; abort if search admits no live search; citation URLs checked against run search hits; router treats empty HTTP 200 as failure and cascades fallbacks.

nodes.label: Prefer short free-tier models for chat
nodes.rationale: Prefer short free-tier models for chat; heavy work through /do planner pipelines.
nodes.norm_label: prefer short free-tier models for chat

nodes.label: Acyclic fallback cascade design
nodes.rationale: Ordered DAG must not form cycles; previous cyclic graph groq→gemini→openrouter→groq raised Cycle detected in fallback chain when Gemini 2.5 failed for new free users. Cerebras is cascade leaf with no cerebras_fallback. Gemini 2.0-flash preferred over 2.5 for free-tier availability.

nodes.label: Skill body injection into chat system prompt
nodes.norm_label: skill body injection into chat system prompt

nodes.label: Skills (policy/workflow) vs Graphify (codebase facts)
nodes.rationale: Free-text chat still uses graphify for codebase facts; enabled skills add policy/workflow on top via truncated system-prompt injection.
